mission.title: Install Network Service Mesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Network Service Mesh (NSM) is a hybrid/multi-cloud IP service mesh that provides advanced L2/L3 networking capabilities to Kubernetes pods. NSM uses SPIRE for workload identity and provides secure, zero-trust network connections between workloads. It is deployed via kustomize overlays from the deployments-k8s repository.

steps.title: Install SPIRE (prerequisite)
steps.description:
NSM requires SPIRE for workload identity. Deploy SPIRE for a single-cluster setup using the NSM-provided kustomize overlay:
```bash
kubectl apply -k https://github.com/networkservicemesh/deployments-k8s/examples/spire/single_cluster?ref=main
```
Wait for SPIRE pods to be ready:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=spire-server -n spire --timeout=120s
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=spire-agent -n spire --timeout=120s
```

steps.title: Deploy NSM core components
steps.description:
Install the NSM core infrastructure (nsmgr, registry, forwarder) using the basic example overlay:
```bash
kubectl apply -k https://github.com/networkservicemesh/deployments-k8s/examples/basic?ref=main
```
This deploys the NSM manager (nsmgr) DaemonSet, the registry, and the kernel forwarder into the `nsm-system` namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all NSM components are running in the nsm-system namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nsm-system
```
You should see:
- `nsmgr-*` pods (DaemonSet, one per node) — the NSM manager
- `registry-*` — the NSM registry
- `forwarder-*` pods — kernel forwarders for dataplane connectivity

Also verify SPIRE is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire
```

steps.title: Test with a sample application (optional)
steps.description:
Deploy a simple NSM client-server example to verify end-to-end connectivity:
```bash
kubectl apply -k https://github.com/networkservicemesh/deployments-k8s/examples/use-cases/Kernel2Kernel?ref=main
```
Verify the client got a network service connection:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=nsc-kernel --timeout=120s
kubectl exec $(kubectl get pod -l app=nsc-kernel -o name) -- ping -c 3 172.16.1.100
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show nsmgr, registry, and forwarder pods running in the nsm-system namespace, and SPIRE pods running in the spire namespace. You can verify connectivity by deploying a Kernel2Kernel example and confirming ping works between NSM-connected pods.

uninstall.title: Remove NSM components
uninstall.description:
Delete the NSM mutating webhook and namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration nsm-mutating-webhook
kubectl delete ns nsm-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove SPIRE
uninstall.description:
Delete the SPIRE components:
```bash
kubectl delete ns spire
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any NSM CRDs if present:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep networkservicemesh | awk '{print $1}')
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade NSM
upgrade.description:
Update the kustomize ref to the desired version tag and re-apply:
```bash
kubectl apply -k https://github.com/networkservicemesh/deployments-k8s/examples/basic?ref=<new-version>
```
Note: Check the NSM releases page for available versions.

troubleshooting.title: SPIRE agent not ready
troubleshooting.description:
NSM components depend on SPIRE for identity. If nsmgr pods are crashing, check SPIRE first:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire
kubectl logs -n spire -l app=spire-agent
```
Ensure the SPIRE server and agent are both running before deploying NSM.

troubleshooting.title: nsmgr pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Check the nsmgr logs for connection errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n nsm-system -l app=nsmgr
```
Common causes: SPIRE not running, insufficient RBAC permissions, or node resource constraints.

troubleshooting.title: Webhook blocking pod creation
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are failing to create with webhook errors:
```bash
kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfiguration nsm-mutating-webhook -o yaml
```
The NSM webhook injects sidecars. If NSM is not running properly, the webhook may block pod creation. As a workaround, delete the webhook: `kubectl delete mutatingwebhookconfiguration nsm-mutating-webhook`

troubleshooting.title: Network connectivity issues between NSM pods
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kernel2Kernel example ping fails:
1. Check forwarder pods are running: `kubectl get pods -n nsm-system -l app=forwarder`
2. Check nsmgr logs for connection errors
3. Verify no network policies are blocking traffic between nsm-system and application namespaces

prerequisites.description: Kubernetes 1.24+ cluster with kubectl configured. SPIRE will be installed as part of this guide. Nodes need to support kernel-level networking for the forwarder. kubectl must support kustomize (built-in since kubectl 1.14).
